revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Futodama]][[it:Futodama]][[ja:フトダマ]][[ru:Футодама]][[simple:Futodama]][[zh:布刀玉命]]


{{short description|Japanese deity}}{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| name = Futodama
| deity_of = God of Rituals,Oracle and Divination
| parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|1=Kamimusubi}}; {{ill|Q97184246|qid=Q97184246|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}}| image =file:Futodama.jpg; File:Futodama.jpg| children = [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Phoebe (Greek myth)|Phoebe]]
| siblings = [[Omoikane|Omoikane]], [[Takuhatachiji-hime]]
| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}
| consort = {{ill|Q85879124|qid=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}}
| offspring = {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|qid=Q462803|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}; {{ill|Kamo Taketsunomi|qid=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q110064736|qid=Q110064736|1=Ame no Kushimimi}}; {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|qid=Q24865597|1=Ōmiya-no-Me}}
}}
'''Futodama''' ({{lang|ja|[[:ja:フトダマ|布刀玉命]]}}) is a god in [[Japanese mythology]], claimed to be the ancestor of [[Inbe clan|Imbe clan]], whose characteristics are believed to reflect the functions of the clan as court ritualists.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Futodama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=52|access-date=2021-07-13|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

== Name and etymology ==
The god is known as Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto ({{lang|ja|天太玉命}}) or Futodama ({{lang|ja|布刀玉}}, {{lang|ja|太玉}}) for short. His name is speculated to mean great gift or offering.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|last=Aston|first=W. G.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=DNPOCgAAQBAJ&q=futodama+name&pg=PT135|title=Shinto - The Ancient Religion of Japan|date=2015-11-16|publisher=Read Books Ltd|isbn=978-1-4733-7719-6|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Aston|first=William George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=nyUNAAAAYAAJ&q=divination+futodama&pg=PA184|title=Shinto, the Way of the Gods|date=1905|publisher=Longmans, Green|isbn=9780524006801|language=en}}</ref>

== Myths ==
After [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] accidentally killed one of [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu's]] attendants in her weaving hall, she got upset and locked herself in [[Amano-Iwato]] causing the world to plunge into darkness, so [[Omoikane|Omoikane]] and other gods came up with a plan to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Amaterasu|url=https://mythopedia.com/japanese-mythology/gods/amaterasu/|access-date=2021-07-13|website=Mythopedia|language=en}}</ref> Futodama and [[Ame-no-Koyane|Amenokoyane]] were tasked with performing a {{ill|Divination|en|Divination|qid=Q1043197|lt=Divination|1=Divination}}. After [[Amaterasu]] left the cave, Futodama used a {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|qid=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}} to prevent her from going back to the cave again. This story is said to be the mythical origin of {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|qid=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}}.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Bocking|first=Brian|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=MWGQAgAAQBAJ&q=Shimenawa|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2005-09-30|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-135-79738-6|language=en}}</ref>

In ''[[Kogo Shūi|Kogo shūi]]'', Futodama is placed as the leader of the preformed rituals.<ref name=":2" />

== Family ==
{{Further information|Family Tree of Japanese deities}}

{{Tree chart/start|align=center|summary=Futodama family tree}}
{{tree chart| Tak | | | | | Ame |Tak=[[Takamimusubi]]|Ame=[[Amaterasu]]}}
{{tree chart| |!| | | | | |!}}
{{tree chart| |)|-|-|.| | |!}}
{{tree chart| Fu  | Ta  |y| Oh  |Fu='''Futodama'''|Ta=[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]|Oh={{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt= Oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}}}
{{tree chart| |!| | | | |!}}
{{tree chart| In  | | | Ni  |In=[[Inbe clan]]|Ni=[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]}}
{{Tree chart/end}}

In [[Kogo Shūi|Kogo shūi]] Futodama is the child of [[Takuhadachiji-hime|Takuhatachijihime]], and grandchild of [[Takamimusubi]].{{cn|date=January 2021|reason=English translation on JHTI doesn't seem to agree.}} But in many versions he is the son of Takamimusubi and the uncle to [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]].<ref>{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane|first=R. A. B.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=BY23AwAAQBAJ&q=futotama+cousins&pg=PA11|title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines|date=2014-06-03|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-89294-3|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2" />

== Worship ==

Futodama is believed to be enshrined at [[Awa Shrine|Awa shrine]], where there is a festival to the kami every year on August 10.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&dq=Awa+shrine&pg=PA61|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2002|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|language=en}}</ref>  He is also enshrined at {{ill|Amatsu Shrine|en|Amatsu Shrine|qid=Q172253|lt=Amatsu Shrine|1=Amatsu Shrine}} alongside [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] and [[Ame-no-Koyane]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kotodamaya - Amatsu Jinja|url=http://www.kotodamaya.com/amatsu-jinja/|access-date=2021-08-17|website=www.kotodamaya.com| date=21 January 2009 |language=ja}}</ref>  The ''[[Engishiki|Engi Shiki]]'' lists several shrines to Futodama in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}.{{Citation needed|date=May 2024}}

== Popular culture ==
In the Japanese {{ill|Role-playing video game|en|Role-playing video game|qid=Q744038|lt=role playing game|1=Role-playing video game}} ''{{ill|Shin Megami Tensei IV|en|Shin Megami Tensei IV|qid=Q3959542|lt=Shin Megami Tensei IV|1=Shin Megami Tensei IV}}'' Futotama is a level 42 demon. Futotama creates other demons when fused; if he is fused with Virtue it creates Israfel, and if Futotama is fused with Master Therion it creates Vivian.<ref>{{Cite book | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=BJHiCgAAQBAJ&q=Futotama | isbn=9781631027192 | title=Shin Megami Tensei IV - Strategy Guide | date=7 November 2015 | publisher=Gamer Guides }}</ref>

== References ==
